steps.title: Find the contrast signal
steps.content: The notes include the contrast word “however” when describing Crosfield’s work, highlighting a difference in what each scientist studied.

steps.title: Match the signal to the choice
steps.content: Choice C uses a semicolon and the conjunctive adverb “however” to directly contrast Vickers’s study of algae in the Canary Islands with Crosfield’s study of the hills of Northeast Wales. The other choices either talk about the general value of historical research, list fields without contrasting the two scientists, or note only their shared contributions to science. That matches choice C — C is correct.
